summary.highest_value_theme: Pre-execution governance as the category-defining inversion

candidates.source_excerpt: Most AI platforms treat governance as a wrapper bolted on after the model acts. Keon inverts that: every action proposed by an agent is evaluated against a policy graph before execution.
candidates.marketing_angle: Pre-execution governance as the category-defining inversion — governance is the runtime, not a wrapper.
candidates.suggested_rewrite: Governance shouldn't be a wrapper around AI. Keon evaluates every agent action against a policy graph before it runs.

red_flags.source_excerpt: We are also building a receipts pipeline that will emit a signed artifact for every governed action; the first version ships in Q3.
red_flags.issue: Receipts pipeline is roadmap, not shipped. Risk of being quoted as a current capability if extracted without context.
red_flags.safer_wording: Signed receipts for every governed action are on the Q3 roadmap.
red_flags.proof_requirement: Do not present as current capability until the Q3 release ships and a sample signed receipt is published.
